title: Latency, Throughput, Memory, Caching, Load, and Cost · Program Brief: Rag Total Cost Accounting
summary: A team optimizes average response time with a warm cache, ignores index memory and model queueing, retrieves more candidates on every request, and reports token price without total service cost. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the total cost accounting increment in RAG capacity and cost envelope. Learners pr…
objectives: Account for ingestion, parsing, OCR, embeddings, storage, index build, query, reranking, generation, network, observability, evaluation, operations, and recovery.
Measure embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, prefix, and answer cache hit rate, saved work, staleness, authorization, invalidation, and quality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example RAG engineering team handles case 3727 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to budget stage latency, throughput, memory, provider work, cache validity, and cost without hiding quality loss; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Account for ingestion, parsing, OCR, embeddings, storage, index build, query, reranking, generation, network, observability, evaluation, operations, and recovery. A defensible solution applies Rag Total Cost Accounting, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to RAG capacity and cost envelope.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-performance-cost-capacity-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-performance-cost-capacity-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_performance_cost_capacity_4(queries_per_second: int = 24, retrieval_ms: int = 45, rerank_ms: int = 38, generation_ms: int = 420, deadline_ms: int = 650, cost_microunits: int = 950, cache_hit: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if queries_per_second <= 0 or min(retrieval_ms, rerank_ms, generation_ms) < 0:
        return False, 0, "capacity-input"
    total_ms = retrieval_ms + rerank_ms + generation_ms
    if total_ms > deadline_ms:
        return False, total_ms, "latency-budget"
    if cost_microunits < 0:
        return False, cost_microunits, "cost-accounting"
    adjusted_cost = cost_microunits // 2 if cache_hit else cost_microunits
    assert adjusted_cost <= cost_microunits
    return True, adjusted_cost, "capacity-envelope"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python RAG decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected query or corpus case, then state which native model, vector-store, provider, network, load, privacy, security, recovery, or deployment claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Total Cost Accounting model · Theory Rag Total Cost Accounting
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Total Cost Accounting: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 4111 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to budget stage latency, throughput, memory, provider work, cache validity, and cost without hiding quality loss; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Account for ingestion, parsing, OCR, embeddings, storage, index build, query, reranking, generation, network, observability, evaluation, operations, and recovery.
